summary: Bearer-only regression verified: with the frontend axios now sending ONLY `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (withCredentials=false), all NUMA backend endpoints continue to work. 17/17 tests pass in /app/backend/tests/test_bearer_only_regression.py. Verified: auth (login/register/me/logout) returns token in body and accepts Bearer-only requests; CORS preflight (OPTIONS) + cross-origin POST return ACAO; seed course 'Plantas Medicinales 101' has 3 lessons + 2 resources + quiz + assignment; quiz correct_index is hidden BEFORE submission and revealed AFTER (answers [1,2,3,1] => 100%); BADGES now returns Spanish names (Primeros Pasos, Pionero, Maestro del Quiz, Estrella Naciente, Erudito, Polímata) with predominantly green palette; /api/me/upcoming filters out submitted activities, returns course meta, and sorts ascending by due_date; teacher grade endpoint persists score (verified via GET /me/submissions). The previously-flagged BADGES localization issue from iteration_2 is FIXED.

minor.issue: Response body is `{"ok": true}` only. Returning the updated submission (with score+feedback+graded_by) would let the UI update without an extra round-trip.

Token is returned under key `token` in the body, but the convention for OAuth-style responses is `access_token` (with `token_type: "bearer"`). Not breaking — frontend reads `token` — but worth noting for API consistency.

Carryover from iter_1: allow_credentials=True + allow_origins='*' is technically invalid per CORS spec. Browsers will refuse credentialed requests. Since the frontend is now Bearer-only (no cookies), this is largely a non-issue — but the backend still set-cookies on login/logout, so any future cookie-based flow would silently break in browsers. Recommend either dropping allow_credentials=True or setting CORS_ORIGINS to an explicit list.
Carryover from iter_2: Pydantic body still requires activity_id which is redundant with the path param. Tests must duplicate it.
Carryover from iter_2: does not filter out activities whose due_date is already in the past. Still returns them as 'upcoming'.
Carryover from iter_1: publicly downloadable, no auth dependency. Unchanged.

action_items: (Optional) Return the updated submission object from POST /api/submissions/{id}/grade instead of {ok:true} to avoid a second round-trip.
(Optional) Standardize login/register response keys to {access_token, token_type:'bearer', user} per OAuth convention.
(Optional, carryover) Tighten CORS: drop allow_credentials=True OR set CORS_ORIGINS to an explicit list; current '*' + credentials is invalid for browsers.
(Optional, carryover) Drop activity_id from QuizSubmissionIn / SubmissionFileIn bodies — already in path.
(Optional, carryover) /api/me/upcoming: also exclude activities whose due_date is in the past.
(Optional, carryover) Add auth dependency to GET /api/files/{file_id} so uploaded files aren't world-readable.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 796 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline; consider splitting routes into routers (auth/courses/activities/submissions/stats) for maintainability.

get_current_user accepts BOTH cookie and Bearer. Since the frontend has dropped cookies, the cookie path is now dead UX-wise but still functional. Keeping both is fine for backwards compatibility but document it.
set_auth_cookie uses secure=False and samesite='lax'. If you ever re-enable cookie auth in production over HTTPS with a cross-site frontend, this needs secure=True + samesite='none'.
BADGES list is hardcoded Spanish strings. For future i18n, return ids only and let frontend translate, or maintain a localization map.
test_credentials: admin@eduquest.com / admin123 (seeded teacher). Test students created at runtime: TEST_numa_stu_<uuid>@example.com / Secret123!
seed_data_creation: Test creates a fresh student per session and 3 dated TEST_ activities under 'Plantas Medicinales 101' for due_date sorting; the activities are cleaned up at the end of test_16 via DELETE /api/activities/{id}. Test student accounts remain in DB (no /users DELETE endpoint exists).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bearer-only auth fully verified end-to-end. The frontend can safely drop cookies; backend works on Authorization header alone. CORS preflight & cross-origin POST also verified. iteration_2's BADGES Spanish-localization gap is now fixed and asserted in test_14. The new comprehensive Bearer suite is /app/backend/tests/test_bearer_only_regression.py — re-run that plus test_numa_new_endpoints.py to cover the surface area. Known-but-acceptable items: CORS '*' + credentials (browser will block credentialed mode but Bearer flow is unaffected), public /api/files/{file_id} download, /me/upcoming not filtering past-due.
rca of the issue: No bug found this iteration. All backend functionality is intact under Bearer-only auth. Initial test failures were test-side: (a) backend returns the JWT under key `token`, not `access_token`; (b) my-courses endpoint is `/api/courses/mine`, not `/api/me/courses`; (c) POST /grade returns {ok:true} rather than the updated submission. Tests were corrected to match the actual API contract.
